action.reasoning: Observation: Previous training attempts consistently OOM when loading full-precision model. The earlier LoRA run (500 samples) gave grounding_score=0.0, likely due to insufficient training and maybe low rank. Analysis: QLoRA (4-bit quantization) drastically reduces GPU memory, enabling training with larger datasets and higher LoRA rank without OOM. bitsandbytes is installed from earlier iteration. Training with batch_size=1 and gradient accumulation on a single GPU should be feasible. Need to implement QLoRA training, monitor val IoU, merge adapter to full precision, and save to checkpoints/merged_model for eval. Decision: Write new train.py with QLoRA (4-bit), run a short training (max_steps=800) on 3000 samples to measure time and check val IoU. If val IoU shows improvement (>0.05), extend training and submit eval. Otherwise, diagnose output format issues.
